trip.title: Big Island 2026
trip.subtitle: Three travel-light itineraries built around your bookings. Tap any activity to see the details & real family reviews.

events.name: Costco grocery run
events.drive: ~53 min · north toward Kona (on the arrival route)
events.blurb: Stock the estate for the week on the drive in from the airport — it's about 10 min from KOA and roughly on the way south. Grab beach snacks, breakfast, and dinner basics.

events.name: The Vanillerie vanilla tour
events.booking: Reserve · tours Tue–Fri 9am–2pm · Sat 9am–12pm · closed Mon & Sun
events.blurb:
Family-owned vanilla farm 5 min from the airport. ~1-hour guided walk through the orchard and shade houses — see, smell and taste vanilla, ending with a scoop of vanilla bean ice cream. Easy and low-effort.

Tour times
• Tue & Fri — 10am · 12pm · 2pm
• Wed & Thu — 10am · 12pm
• Sat — 10am · 12pm
• Sun & Mon — closed
Your flight lands ~12:33pm Wed, after the last Wednesday tour — so it can't be the arrival-day stop. A Friday or Saturday morning slot fits best.

reviews.quote: A family of 6 took the tour and were all blown away; kids around 10 and 12 enjoyed it too.
A perfect low-effort farm tour for families with older children — though a couple of reviewers said it's less suited to very young kids.

events.name: Puʻuhonua o Hōnaunau (Place of Refuge)
events.blurb: Sacred royal grounds with carved kiʻi, fishponds and a 1,000-ft stone wall. Flat, paved, shaded coastal paths make it the most grandma-friendly stop on the island, and green sea turtles often bask in the cove. Pairs perfectly with Two Step next door.

reviews.quote: Flat, paved paths make it easy for everyone, and turtles are often resting right along the shore — a calm, beautiful, shaded walk.
Ranger talks at 10:30am and 2:30pm; morning visits beat the afternoon heat.

events.name: Two Step snorkel (Honaunau Bay)
events.ages: Adults + confident kids; little ones in the shallow left side
events.booking: Walk-in · open 24 hrs, free · go before 9am
events.blurb: One of the island's best snorkels — clear water, tons of reef fish, frequent turtles and sometimes dolphins. Entry is off lava 'steps' (no sand), so wear water shoes and go at lower tide in the morning.

reviews.quote: The best snorkeling! You will see all the fish! We've had the good luck of being in the water the same time a pod of dolphins came in.
There's a very shallow area to the left of the steps where young kids can splash safely while the adults snorkel the deeper reef.

events.name: Teshima's — saimin (Grandma's pick!)
events.blurb: A nearly 100-year-old (est. 1929) family-run Japanese-American institution on the highway between the estate and Kona — the ideal spot for Grandma's saimin, and they also open early for omelettes and a Hawaii-style Japanese breakfast. Big local portions, old-Hawaii charm.

reviews.quote: Their saimin is the real deal — light, flavorful, and deeply satisfying; served in an old-fashioned cast-iron pot.
Old-school, no-frills Japanese comfort food that's been serving Big Island locals longer than almost anywhere.

events.name: Hoʻokena Beach Park
events.booking: Walk-in · park 7am–7pm · go early for parking
events.blurb: Your closest beach — a salt-and-pepper sand cove with shade, restrooms, an outdoor shower, and spinner dolphins resting in the bay most mornings. The access road is narrow and steep; no lifeguard.

reviews.quote: Hidden gem, lovely salt-and-pepper sand beach… Kid friendly, nice showers, bathroom close by. Parking can be limiting.
The waves were not too overwhelming… the current can be a bit strong but you can easily stay close to shore and wade. Good sandcastle sand.

events.name: Greenwell Farms coffee tour
events.blurb: Free, no-reservation guided walk through a working Kona coffee farm with generous tastings — 8–10 roasts including a chemical-free Swiss Water decaf, so non–coffee drinkers aren't left out. Heads-up for your crew: it's a coffee-focused walking tour, so the 6- and 9-year-olds may drift before the end. What helps — the decaf to sip, plus the gift shop's chocolate-covered Kona coffee beans and macadamia nuts. If you'd rather something hands-on for the kids, see Big Island Bees or the chocolate factory below.

reviews.quote: Free, short and friendly, with lots of Kona coffee — and a decaf — to sample at the end.
It's coffee-focused, so our younger kids lost interest before the end; the chocolate-covered beans in the shop saved us.

events.name: Kahaluʻu Beach — turtle snorkeling
events.ages: Best beginner snorkel for kids; grandma can wade/sit
events.blurb: A lava breakwater keeps this bay shallow and calm — the most reliable spot to snorkel with green sea turtles. Lifeguards, restrooms, showers, shaded benches and 'Reef Teacher' volunteers. Bring water shoes (rocky entry).

reviews.quote: It was easy enough for my 82-year-old grandmother to have a good time too! … We even saw a turtle!
Our favorite vacation as a family. Turtles swimming around… easy access from your car. Kid friendly.

events.name: Ululani's Hawaiian Shave Ice (Grandma's pick!)
events.blurb: Top-rated shave ice in Kona, on Palani Rd in the King Kam hotel — fluffy ice and a huge flavor list (from strawberry to li hing mui and Thai tea). Easy to pair with an Aliʻi Drive stroll.

reviews.quote: The shave ice is great and portions are huge — a keiki (kids) size is plenty for one person.
Kids and extended family found it a highlight of the trip; tons of flavors to choose from.

events.name: Kamakahonu (King Kam) Beach
events.ages: Calmest water — perfect for the youngest & grandma
events.blurb: The calmest, most protected cove on the coast, right in Kailua-Kona town by the pier — sand-bottom and just a few feet deep. Gear rentals on the sand, restrooms close, steps from restaurants and shops.

reviews.quote: Very small public beach with soft sand… It's very protected so a great place for kids. The snorkeling is actually pretty good!
One of the calmest places to get in the ocean… you can rent umbrellas, chairs and snorkel gear right on the beach.

events.name: Island Breeze Luau (BOOKED)
events.blurb: Kona's favorite luau at the King Kamehameha Beach Hotel — Royal Court arriving by outrigger canoe, an imu (underground oven) ceremony, open bar, big buffet, and a Polynesian show with a fire dancer. Hula lessons and crafts keep the kids busy.

reviews.quote: The hula lessons before the show kept the kids entertained, and the buffet had plenty of kid-friendly options.
A great display of Hawaiian culture — dances, graceful hula and a spectacular fire dancer; staff are very accommodating with dietary needs.

events.name: July 4th Parade & Fireworks
events.blurb:
Kailua-Kona's hometown 4th of July: a parade down to Aliʻi Drive at 6pm and fireworks over Kailua Bay at 8pm. Book an oceanfront dinner (e.g. Fish Hopper) with a 5:00–5:30 seating to watch from your table — these fill weeks ahead, and bring a flashlight for the walk back.

Quieter places to watch, away from the pier crowds:
• Old Kona Airport park — lovely for a daytime/sunset beach hangout, but the gate closes at 8pm (7am–8pm daily), right when the fireworks start — so it won't work for the show itself.
• Upcountry Holualoa (~10–15 min up Hualālai Rd) — quiet, breezy, panoramic distant view over the bay.
• South seawall near Huggoʻs / Royal Kona Resort — clear bay view with a thinner crowd than the pier.
• On the water — fireworks boat cruises (Body Glove catamaran, Kona Explorer raft), though both currently look sold out for 2026 (Body Glove may have a waitlist). Boats stay outside the 600-ft barge zone.
Heads-up: from the estate in South Kona there's no sightline to the bay, so plan to watch from town.

events.name: Kona Glass-Bottom Boat (BOOKED)
events.ages: All ages — no swimming, stay dry
events.booking: BOOKED · Sun 7/5, 12:15–1:05pm · check-in 12:00 at Kailua Pier (halfway down, left side) · #352792326 · sign waivers for ALL passengers beforehand · park at King Kam lot — a purchase at Ululani's validates
events.blurb: A 50-minute narrated reef tour through glass viewing windows — no age minimum, no physical requirements, nobody gets wet. The easiest possible way for Grandma and non-swimmers to see the reef. It leaves from the Kailua-Kona pier, only ~15 min from the airport, so it could even work as an arrival-day activity if you can make the 2:45 departure (Wed is an open day).

reviews.quote: Stay completely dry while watching the reef through the windows — reviewers call it ideal for grandparents and little kids.
Short, narrated and easy to fit into a town day; a relaxed alternative to a full snorkel cruise.

events.name: Atlantis Submarine Kona
events.booking: Book ahead (sells out) · Mon–Sat 8am–4pm · closed Sun
events.blurb: A real 48-passenger submarine descends ~100 ft to a coral reef and sunken ships — air-conditioned, narrated, no swimming. A huge hit with kids and a great option for non-swimmers (Grandma included, if she can manage the entry ladder).

reviews.quote: My 3- and 7-year-olds had a blast… we saw so many colorful fish, shipwrecks and coral reefs. I'm usually claustrophobic but I felt very comfortable. Worth every penny!
A great way for my 4-year-old to see the ocean floor… with children it was certainly worth doing. A great memory for her.

events.name: Fair Wind snorkel cruise — Kealakekua Bay
events.ages: All ages; grandma stays on the shaded deck
events.booking: Book 4–6 wks ahead · open daily 8am–5pm (Keauhou Bay)
events.blurb: A big, stable catamaran to the island's premier marine sanctuary, with easy staircase water entry, two waterslides and a high-jump platform. Breakfast and lunch included, plus gear. Younger kids can use boogie boards with viewing windows.

reviews.quote: The kids (7 and 9) found the slides to be a big hit, and the crew gave beginner snorkelers instruction before opening up the slides and diving board.
Boogie boards with window-like viewing portholes let younger children watch the fish while staying safely on the surface.

events.name: Ocean Rider Seahorse Farm
events.blurb: The world's only seahorse aqua-farm — a guided, interactive tour led by marine biologists where kids 5+ can have a seahorse wrap around their finger. Note: weekdays only, can be pricey and crowded with big groups.

reviews.quote: Engaging and interactive for both young and old, with the highlight being the chance to 'hold' a seahorse at the end of the tour.
Staff were exceptionally knowledgeable and friendly — kids loved it; a couple of reviewers noted big groups can feel rushed.

events.name: Hapuna Beach (north day)
events.booking: Walk-in · 7am–6:30pm · paid parking (gate closes when full)
events.blurb: A half-mile of wide golden sand with gentle summer waves and lifeguards — the most kid- and grandma-friendly of the far-north beaches. Bring your own shade and food; no concessions.

reviews.quote: Wide, soft sand with easy entry and lifeguards — consistently rated one of the best beaches in the country.
A true beach day for everyone; just get there early in summer before the lot fills.

events.name: Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park
events.drive: ~1 hr 30 min · south toward the volcano (via Nāʻālehu)
events.ages: Everyone; easy paved overlooks + Junior Ranger for kids
events.booking: Park open 24 hrs · $30/vehicle · (Kīlauea Visitor Center temporarily closed)
events.blurb: The island's must-do day trip: steaming craters, lava fields, rainforest and the Thurston Lava Tube. Stick to the easy paved stops (Uēkahuna overlook, Sulphur Banks boardwalk, the lava tube). Kids 7–12 love the Junior Ranger program. Pack layers — it's 10–15°F cooler.

reviews.quote: One minute you are walking through lush fern rainforest, the next across a barren lava field… If you're visiting the Big Island, this is an absolute must-do.
Walking through the lava tube was really cool and the scale of the lava flows is amazing. Well worth the $30 entry — we spent about 5 hours.

events.name: Punaluʻu Black Sand Beach
events.drive: ~1 hr · south toward the volcano (pairs with Volcano day)
events.ages: Everyone — viewing only, not for swimming
events.blurb: Jet-black sand where green sea turtles bask midday — an easy, memorable stop on the way to/from the volcano. Not a swimming beach (rocky, strong currents). Stay 20 ft from turtles; the sand gets hot, so wear shoes. Punaluʻu Bake Shop nearby for famous malasadas.

reviews.quote: Kids loved the sea turtles, and also playing in the tide pools trying to catch little fish — half a dozen turtles sunning on the beach.
Easy access, parking and restrooms; a genuinely memorable stop if you're already heading to the volcano.

events.name: Big Island Bees — honey farm tour
events.booking: Reserve · tours Mon–Fri 10am–3pm · closed Sat & Sun
events.blurb: A genuinely fun ~90-min honey farm tour right near the estate: watch an open-hive demo from behind a screen (find the queen!), taste several honey varietals, and browse the little bee museum. Sweet, hands-on and a hit with kids — and since you're tasting honey, not coffee, the whole crew stays into it.

reviews.quote: Educational and fun family tour — the kids loved the farm and sampling the honey.
They make it very easy for families with little kids; the open-hive demo and honey tasting kept everyone engaged.

events.name: Original Hawaiian Chocolate Factory
events.ages: Best for ~6+ (little ones may get restless)
events.booking: Reserve · Tue–Fri 10am–3pm · closed Mon, Sat & Sun
events.blurb: A bean-to-bar cacao farm tour led by the owner himself — see a fresh cacao pod cut open, follow the beans through drying and roasting, and finish tasting milk, dark and rare criollo chocolate. Short, mostly shaded, with chairs at each stop. Your 9/11/13-year-olds will love it; the 6-year-old may need the chocolate payoff to stay patient.

reviews.quote: All ages enjoyed it, though very young kids may get restless since it's not highly interactive — the chocolate tasting at the end is the big payoff.
Bob leads the tour himself; it's small, short and mostly shaded with chairs along the way — easy and genuinely interesting.

events.name: South Kona Fruit Stand — smoothies
events.blurb: A roadside organic fruit stand near the estate with some of the best tropical smoothies around (pineapple–passion fruit, papaya–banana), plus fresh fruit and sandwiches. A perfect sweet, healthy pit-stop on a south-Kona day — the owner will happily explain the funky fruits to the kids.

reviews.quote: The kids loved the smoothies and sandwiches, and the owner happily explained all the different fruits.
Some of the best smoothies ever — all organic, grown on their own farm.

events.name: Punaluʻu Bake Shop — malasadas
events.drive: ~46 min · south toward the volcano (pairs with Volcano day)
events.blurb: The southernmost bakery in the U.S. and the classic sweet stop on a volcano day — warm Portuguese malasadas (fried sugar-dusted dough) in flavors like guava, lilikoʻi and chocolate, plus smoothies and coffee. Pro tip: order one of every flavor and cut them up so everyone gets a taste.

reviews.quote: Our two little girls were all smiles — they said it was one of their favorite stops on the whole trip.
Order one of every malasada flavor and cut them into pieces so everyone gets a taste.

events.name: The Coffee Shack
events.booking: 7am–3:30pm daily · closed Wed · NO reservations — call (808) 328-9555 about an hour before you want to sit to join the waitlist; they text when the table is ready. Arrive close to your quoted time (tight parking, 1 handicap space out front) and carpool with a group this size
events.blurb: A South Kona institution on an open-air lanai with a sweeping view over Kealakekua Bay — the closest sit-down breakfast to the house. Island-style breakfast and lunch: eggs benedict, French toast on house-baked island bread, big omelettes, plus their own Kona coffee.

reviews.quote: Fantastic food with big, satisfying portions — the eggs benedict and French toast on island bread were delicious, and the view is five stars.
The warm fresh coconut pound cake is a must, and the coffee is fabulous.

events.name: Kaʻaloa's Super J's — Hawaiian food
events.booking: Walk-in · 10am–3pm Mon & Wed–Sat · closed Tue & Sun (can sell out)
events.blurb: A tiny, family-run spot famous for pork laulau slow-cooked for hours, plus kalua pig and lomi salmon, served at family-style tables. Featured on Food Network's 'The Best Thing I Ever Ate' — the real-deal local plate lunch, minutes from the house.

reviews.quote: The pork was so tender it was like cutting through butter with a hot knife — the best laulau we found on the island.
The pork lau lau had been slow-cooked for 12 hours and you could taste it — incredible flavor.

events.name: South Kona Grindz
events.blurb: A casual, family-friendly local spot for plate lunches and comfort food — fish & chips, fried shrimp, and local plates. An easy stop on the way home from the Big Island Bees tour, just south toward the house.

events.name: Rebel Kitchen
events.blurb: A casual, contemporary spot in Kealakekua with Hawaiian-inspired burgers, sandwiches, and plates, plus a strong vegetarian & vegan selection. Open daily into the evening — an easy near-home dinner.

events.name: Kona International Airport (KOA)
events.booking: UA 1722 lands 12:33pm Wed Jul 1 · departs 12:00pm Tue Jul 7
events.blurb: Open-air island airport just north of Kailua-Kona. Land, grab the rental car (Hertz on-site), and the trip is on.

events.name: On the Rocks — oceanfront lunch
events.blurb: Casual open-air spot right on the water next to Huggo's in Kailua-Kona — fish tacos, burgers and island plates with the surf a few feet away. An easy first stop ~20 min from the airport.

events.name: Safeway grocery run (Kailua-Kona)
events.blurb: Full-size Safeway up on Henry St, ~6 min from lunch — stock the estate for the week, then make the drive south to the house.

events.name: Kona Coffee Living History Farm
events.ages: Everyone — easy, flat walking · great for grandma
events.booking: Open Tue & Fri ONLY, 10am–2pm — on this trip that means Fri Jul 3
events.blurb: A preserved 1920s Kona coffee farm (the D. Uchida homestead) run by the Kona Historical Society — costumed interpreters demonstrate the crafts and daily life of the Japanese immigrant families who built the Kona coffee industry. The one true museum experience in South Kona.

events.name: St. Benedict's Painted Church
events.booking: Grounds open daily ~7am–5pm (visitor hours Tue–Sat) · pairs perfectly with Place of Refuge / Two Step
events.blurb: The famous little 1899 church above Hōnaunau whose entire interior is covered in folk-art murals — a Belgian priest painted the walls and ceiling to teach Bible stories to Hawaiians who couldn't read. Five minutes from Place of Refuge.

reviews.quote: Beautiful small church. Worth a visit to see the historic art on the walls. And if attending Mass, you will be enthusiastically welcomed by parishioners.
We visited this on our way back from the City of Refuge. It's a neat little stop if you like a bit of history… The inside is painted entirely with house paint.

events.name: Paleaku Gardens Peace Sanctuary (Galaxy Garden)
events.ages: Everyone — shaded paths for grandma, Galaxy Garden for kids
events.blurb: A quiet 9-acre botanical garden on Painted Church Rd with shrines from many world traditions — and its showpiece, the Galaxy Garden: a walkable scale model of the Milky Way planted entirely in flowers.

reviews.quote: Loved the variety of plants and the true zen feelings of this peaceful garden. Easily manageable walk with great ocean views… A wonderful way to spend a couple of hours. Very reasonably priced.
They just put in a butterfly garden and all the butterflies were around. It was very relaxing. I highly recommend the visit.

events.name: Amy Greenwell Ethnobotanical Garden
events.blurb: Native Hawaiian plants and traditional pre-contact Kona field-system agriculture, lovingly revived by a community group. A peaceful hour among kalo, ʻulu and endemic species you won't see anywhere else.

events.name: Hala Tree Coffee — organic farm tour
events.blurb: A smaller, organic Kona coffee farm with personal tours and tastings — the boutique alternative to Greenwell Farms if you want coffee without the tour-bus crowd.

events.name: Kona Boys — Kealakekua Bay kayak tours
events.ages: Adults & strong swimmers — the monument paddle is a workout
events.booking: Mon–Sat 7am–12pm · Sun 9:30am–2pm · book ahead (permits required for monument landings)
events.blurb: The legal, guided way to kayak across Kealakekua Bay to the Captain Cook Monument and snorkel the island's best reef — Kona Boys holds the landing permits. Morning departures; pairs with the Manini/Napoʻopoʻo side of the bay for the rest of the group.

events.name: Kula Kai Caverns — lava tube tours
events.drive: ~29 min · south toward the volcano (Ocean View)
events.cost: Lighted Trail tour $35 · longer tours more
events.ages: Lighted-trail tour suits kids & grandma · longer crawls for adventurers
events.booking: Tue–Sun 9am–4pm · closed Mon · 2-hr tours, must book ahead
events.blurb: Guided tours through a centuries-old lava-tube system under Ocean View — easy lighted walkways or full caving options. It's on the south route, so it bolts onto a volcano day or makes its own half-day.

reviews.quote: William was a fantastic tour guide and it was so much fun to see and learn about the lava flows… My 9 year old loved being able to explore underground — it was totally worth it!
Honestly super informative and Brian was a great tour guide, very kind and very knowledgeable. I recommend doing the longer tour — it's totally worth it.

events.name: Sushi Cocoro & Udon Noodle
events.ages: Everyone — udon is great for the kids
events.blurb: A small sushi-and-udon spot on Aliʻi Drive in Kailua-Kona — fresh fish for the adults, big comforting udon bowls for the kids, all in one stop. Note the midday break: lunch ends 2:30, dinner starts 5.

events.name: Kamakana Playground (Higashihara Park)
events.drive: ~32 min · north toward Kona (Honalo, just past Teshima's)
events.ages: Made for the kids · shaded picnic tables for grandma
events.blurb: Kamakana Playground — the beloved community-built wooden play structure at Higashihara Park — castle towers, bridges and crawl-throughs the kids can burn an hour on. Right on the highway just south of Keauhou, two minutes from Teshima's, so it pairs perfectly with a saimin lunch.

events.name: Honaunau Poke Shop
events.blurb: Fresh-catch poke bowls minutes from Two Step and Place of Refuge — grab bowls after a snorkel morning, or take them down to the water. One of the closest lunch stops to the house.

events.name: Kealia Ranch Store
events.drive: ~8 min · north toward Kona (by the Hoʻokena turnoff)
events.cost: Shave ice ~$5–8 · ranch beef & local goods
events.ages: Everyone — the easiest stop of the whole trip
events.blurb: The little store of a working South Kona cattle ranch, just up the highway from the house — Kealia Ranch grass-fed beef, locally crafted goods, and real-fruit shave ice (get the lilikoi). The closest stop in the planner, and the natural treat after a Hoʻokena beach morning.

reviews.quote: The shave ice here is no joke, get the lilikoi which has real fruit in it. Amazing. The owners are super cool and will not lead you astray.
Great for shaved ice at any time or after a visit to Ho'Okena beach. Friendly service. Small selection of locally crafted items as well as plenty of beef products.

events.name: Da Bread Stand
events.booking: Walk-in · Tue–Fri 10am–2pm ONLY · closed Sat–Mon · goes fast
events.blurb: A tiny roadside bakery stand in Captain Cook with a near-perfect rating — fresh-baked loaves and goodies, four hours a day, four days a week. Right in the Honaunau/Captain Cook corridor, so it stacks onto any near-home morning. Go early; small-batch means sold out is real.

events.name: Banana Bread Spot
events.drive: ~19 min · north toward Kona (Captain Cook, near Kealakekua Bay road)
events.blurb: A little roadside stand with a perfect rating doing one thing well: fresh local banana bread. Open daily with long hours — unlike most of the small South Kona stands — so it works as a grab-on-the-way treat any day of the trip, including weekends.

events.name: Captain James Cook Monument (Kealakekua Bay)
events.drive: ~23 min · north toward Kona (Napoʻopoʻo side) — NO road to the monument itself
events.cost: Free to view · boat tours & guided kayaks vary
events.ages: NOT reachable for grandma on foot — she sees it best from the Fair Wind deck
events.booking: Access: boat tour (easiest), permitted guided kayak (Kona Boys), or a HARD 3.8-mi hike — landings require a state permit
events.blurb: The white obelisk at Kaʻawaloa Cove marks where Captain Cook fell in 1779 — and fronts some of the best snorkeling in Hawaiʻi. There is no road: the Kaʻawaloa Trail is 3.8 mi round trip with ~1,270 ft of climb back out, shadeless and rescue-prone — not for grandma, and a serious outing even for the strong hikers. The family play: Fair Wind cruise anchors right here (grandma watches from the shaded deck while the swimmers snorkel), or the strong-swimmer crew books a permitted Kona Boys kayak tour. Drive-up distant views from Napoʻopoʻo / Manini Beach across the bay.

events.name: Menehune Coffee Company
events.drive: ~16 min · north toward Kona (Honaunau, by the fruit stand)
events.blurb: Local Kona coffee roaster and drive-by espresso stop in Honaunau, right near the South Kona Fruit Stand. Opens at 6am daily — the earliest coffee anywhere near the house, perfect before an early Two Step snorkel or the big volcano-day departure.

events.name: Black Rock Pizza (Captain Cook)
events.ages: Everyone · easy crowd-pleaser for the kids
events.blurb: Highly-rated pizza right in Captain Cook, open daily until 8pm — one of the few near-home DINNER options, and the easy takeout answer for a feed-everyone night back at the estate after a beach day.

events.name: Shaka Tacoz
events.ages: Everyone · kids love tacos, ocean-view patio
events.blurb: Casual street tacos and pork burritos on an ocean-view patio in Captain Cook — an easy, kid-pleasing dinner run from the house. Closes at 8pm, so head up by 6ish for a relaxed sunset dinner.

events.name: Big Jake's Island B-B-Q — CLOSED
events.booking: ❌ PERMANENTLY CLOSED (Google Maps). Replace with Randy's Huli Chicken & Ribs (Kealakekua, Thu–Fri 9am–4pm) or L&L Hawaiian BBQ (Captain Cook, daily)
events.blurb: A roadside BBQ shack where Jake takes your order at the window — smoked brisket, ribs and pulled pork on outdoor picnic tables. Hearty, well-priced, super casual and a hit with kids (and the resident chickens). Closes mid-afternoon, so it's a lunch stop.

reviews.quote: Tender brisket with great bark and a tangy sauce — meat that falls off the bone. Our eight-year-old foodies loved it.
Awesome roadside eats — the full rib rack is immense and easily feeds a family of four.

events.name: Manago Hotel Restaurant
events.booking: Walk-in · open daily ~6am–8pm (closed between meal services)
events.blurb: A century-old plantation-era hotel restaurant and James Beard 'American Classics' winner, famous above all for pan-fried pork chops smothered in onions, served family-style with sides. No frills, very local, very affordable — the quintessential South Kona dinner.

reviews.quote: Best pork chops ever — fried in those old cast-iron skillets, succulent and tender, with enough left over for another whole meal.
Sides are served family style, prices are very reasonable, and the staff are friendly — a true local spot.

events.name: Keʻei Café — CLOSED
events.booking: ❌ PERMANENTLY CLOSED (Google Maps). Try instead Rebel Kitchen (Kealakekua, daily 11am–8pm)
events.blurb: A beloved, understated dinner café serving fresh, homemade island-American cooking — roasted pork chops with peppercorn gravy, fresh-fish specials, and desserts like lilikoʻi cheesecake. Cash-only, no view, no reservations, but locals call it the most consistently great food around. Heads-up for your crew: parties of 7+ get an automatic 20% gratuity.

reviews.quote: From appetizers to dessert, all the food was excellent — the best place for great food in the Kona area, and it never changes.
The roasted pork chops and fresh fish specials are standouts — save room for the lilikoʻi cheesecake.

events.name: Kona Brewing Co.
events.blurb: A relaxed, family-favorite brewpub with a torchlit outdoor lanai and — key for your crew — a kids' play space. Hand-tossed pizzas, pizza rolls, fish tacos, ahi poke and Kalua-pork nachos, plus a Keiki menu (cheese pizza, chicken tenders). Easy, lively dinner on a town day.

reviews.quote: Great pizza and pizza rolls with a cool island vibe — there's an outdoor playspace so the kids run around while you eat.
The hand-tossed pizzas and pork nachos are the move; friendly service and a fun atmosphere with live music.

events.name: Fish Hopper Seafood & Steaks
events.booking: Reserve · open daily 7am–9pm — top July 4th fireworks table
events.blurb: An oceanfront seafood-and-steaks spot on Aliʻi Drive overlooking Kailua Bay, with open-air and window seating. Known for the Hawaiian fresh-fish trio, macadamia-crusted mahi mahi and ahi poke bowls. The top pick for July 4th — book a 5:00–5:30 window table to watch the bay fireworks.

reviews.quote: Macadamia-crusted mahi mahi and the fresh-fish trio were excellent, and the oceanfront view at sunset can't be beat.
Get there before sunset for a window table — it fills up fast and gets lively.

events.name: Da Poke Shack
events.booking: Walk-up takeout · Mon–Fri 10am–4pm · closed Sat & Sun (sells out)
events.blurb: Nationally famous grab-and-go ahi poke — order at the window and take it to the beach. A rotating board of fresh-caught ahi in different seasonings, plus laulau and kalua pork for non-poke eaters. Best as a beach-day lunch; come early, it sells out daily.

reviews.quote: The freshest poke you will ever have — easily the best we tasted in Kona, amazing fish quality and flavor.
Strictly a walk-up takeout spot, and it sells out — go early for the most variety.

events.name: Big Island Grill
events.booking: Walk-in · Tue–Thu 8am–2pm & 4:30–8pm · Fri–Sat 8am–2pm · closed Sun & Mon
events.blurb: A beloved, family-owned spot for big-portion Hawaiian comfort food — loco moco, kalua pork & cabbage, teriyaki beef, Korean chicken and mixed plates, with a keiki menu. Famously huge servings at fair prices; hearty, no-frills and packed with local families.

reviews.quote: The portions are HUGE — enough to share between two or three people — great flavor and great value.
Local grinds, ono food, great value — lots of local families, generous plates, nothing fancy.

events.name: Pine Tree Café
events.blurb: A casual, affordable local café in Captain Cook minutes from the house — hearty breakfasts of loco moco, omelettes, pancakes and fried rice from fresh local ingredients. No frills, generous portions, and it opens early for a quick bite before a beach day.

reviews.quote: Generous portions and fresh, local ingredients — a great-value local breakfast.
A low-key local spot with a strong following; hearty plates and friendly service.

events.name: Island Lava Java
events.blurb: A bustling, family-owned oceanfront café on Aliʻi Drive (now in a bigger two-story spot) with all-day breakfast and big ocean views — fluffy pancakes, breakfast burritos and eggs benedict, made from scratch with fresh local ingredients. The go-to town brunch.

reviews.quote: Breakfast was outstanding — fluffy pancakes and eggs benedict, big portions made from scratch, and the oceanfront view is wonderful.
Staff even made my kid a grilled cheese that wasn't on the menu — friendly, lively and right on the water.

events.name: Standard Bakery
events.booking: Walk-in · Mon–Fri 4:30am–1pm · closed Sat & Sun (sells out)
events.blurb: A beloved family-run bakery in Kainaliu going back to the 1930s — fresh malasadas, the famous HUNKY apple fritters, Chantilly cake, brioche buns and breads, plus bento-box breakfasts and Spam musubi. The closest real bakery to the house; go early, the morning favorites sell out.

reviews.quote: The HUNKY apple fritters are my personal favorite — locals make early-morning trips just for the malasadas.
Freshest, premium ingredients — the Chantilly cake and brioche buns are the top sellers for a reason.

events.name: Kainaliu town stroll (shops & galleries)
events.blurb: A historic plantation town packed into a few walkable blocks — the 1927 Kimura Store (10,000+ bolts of aloha & Japanese fabric), art galleries and gift shops, Paradise Found boutique (aloha wear), Kiernan Music (ukuleles & guitars), and the restored Aloha Theatre & café. A relaxed, grandma-friendly browse near home; pairs nicely with a Keʻei dinner or a Standard Bakery stop.

reviews.quote: An old plantation town with historic Japanese-run shops, eclectic cafés and the restored Aloha Theatre — old-Hawaii charm with a vibrant arts scene.
Kimura's fabric store is a treasure — thousands of bolts of bright Hawaiian and Japanese prints.

events.name: Kealakekua Ranch Center (groceries & supplies)
events.booking: ChoiceMART + Ace Hardware · center open daily ~5am–9:30pm
events.blurb: The closest shopping to the house — a local center in Captain Cook with a ChoiceMART supermarket, Ace Hardware, and a few shops and eateries. Handy for groceries, beach supplies or a forgotten item without the ~50-min drive to Costco in Kona.

events.name: Hana Hou Restaurant (Naʻalehu)
events.blurb: The self-styled 'southernmost restaurant in the USA' in Naʻalehu, right on the volcano route — homemade comfort food since 1940 with local Kaʻū beef and fresh fish: smash burgers, fresh-fish burgers, pizza and a Nashville hot chicken plate. Save room for the famous homemade pies. A friendly, well-priced stop on the drive home from the volcano.

reviews.quote: The macadamia nut cream pie is to die for and the coconut cream pie is fabulous — plus fresh local food at reasonable prices.
A down-home, kid-friendly spot full of locals — the smash burger and fresh fish were great, and it's right on the way to the black sand beach.

events.name: Aliʻi Drive shops & strolling (Kona)
events.blurb: Kailua-Kona's walkable waterfront strip — open-air shops and galleries at the Kona Inn Shopping Village and Coconut Grove, the historic Huliheʻe Palace, outrigger canoes in the bay, and the Kona Farmers Market (Wed–Sun). Easy, flat strolling with shave ice and treats along the way; a natural fit before the luau.

reviews.quote: A pleasant, flat waterfront walk with shops, galleries and ocean views — easy to wander with kids and grandparents.
The Kona Farmers Market and Aliʻi Drive shops are a fun, low-key way to spend an afternoon in town.

events.name: Parrots in Paradise Sanctuary (Airbnb)
events.booking: Airbnb · ~2 hrs, 10am–12pm select days · max 6 guests/slot
events.blurb: A heartfelt parrot-rescue sanctuary in Kealakekua, ~15 min from the house, where hosts Gerald & Dorothy introduce you to 100+ rescued parrots (plus rescue sheep, horses and tortoises). Hold and feed the birds and hear their stories — a gentle, all-ages hit that's great for grandma and animal-loving kids. Heads-up: only 6 guests per session, so your group of 11 would need two back-to-back slots.

reviews.quote: Jerry was a really engaging, knowledgeable and passionate host… super fun to hold them and feed them. Would totally recommend.
Such a sweet experience… only bring children who are gentle and calm, as the birds deserve calm energy. We took our little one and he loved listening to the birds.

events.name: Manta Ray Night Snorkel (Airbnb · Manta Adventures)
events.ages: Ages 7+ · adults & older kids (6-yr-old can't board)
events.blurb: Kona's bucket-list night snorkel — a sunset cruise from Honokōhau Harbor, then float at the surface while giant manta rays glide and barrel-roll under spotlights. Capt. Dan's crew (25+ yrs) handles safety and gear; sometimes dolphins escort the boat on the way out. Beginner-friendly but a moderate water activity: ages 7+ only, so the 6-year-old (and likely grandma) would sit this one out. It can be a full boat in peak season, so book a smaller-group slot if you can.

reviews.quote: Quite possibly the coolest, most otherworldly experience imaginable — one of the top 3 experiences I've ever had. Memories that'll last a lifetime!
Incredible! Dolphins swarmed our boat on the way out and the manta rays were beautiful — our favorite experience of our two-week stay.

events.name: Coconut Experience with The CocoMon (Airbnb)
events.blurb: A fun, hands-on beachside session with host Khali all about the coconut — how to pick, husk and machete one open, then taste fresh coconut water with a beach backdrop. Quirky, educational and a hit with kids; ages 2+ and big enough to take the whole group at once.

reviews.quote: Definitely one of the highlights of our trip in Kona… Khali taught us so many interesting facts. Informative, interactive and lots of fun.
The best event we did all week… I especially enjoyed opening my own coconut and tasting the coconut water.

events.name: Kona Surf Lesson — Ohana Surf (Airbnb)
events.ages: Ages 4+ · all four kids can learn
events.booking: Airbnb · ~2 hrs, multiple times daily · up to 10
events.blurb: A beginner surf lesson at Kahaluʻu Beach with Kaleo's local, family-run Ohana Surf Lessons — lifeguard/CPR-certified instructors, gear provided, and a great instructor-to-student ratio. Ages 4+, so all the kids could try, and they're patient with nervous first-timers (adults included).

reviews.quote: We had never surfed before and were standing in 2–3 waves… great instruction, kept us safe, took us to a local spot. Well worth it!
The kids learned quickly… Kaleo was always there to protect and encourage us. A very enjoyable time.

events.name: Private Kealakekua Bay Snorkel — Capt. Kaleo (Airbnb)
events.drive: ~47 min · north toward Kona (Kailua-Kona boat ramp)
events.booking: Airbnb · ~4.5 hrs · NEW listing (1 review so far)
events.blurb: A small, customizable snorkel charter to Kealakekua Bay & the Captain Cook Monument with Native-Hawaiian captain Kaleo — reef fish, possible dolphins and whales, snacks aboard and lots of Hawaiian history. Only 6 guests per trip (great for a personal feel), so your group of 11 would need two trips. Heads-up: it's a brand-new listing with just one (glowing) review so far.

reviews.quote: Kaleo took our family to the Captain Cook monument — friendly, knowledgeable and genuine. He even found humpback whales and got us in close. A once-in-a-lifetime experience; a smaller boat is nothing but a benefit.

events.name: Mauna Kea Stargazing & Astrophotography (Airbnb)
events.blurb: A small-group night near the base of Mauna Kea with an astronomer and astrophotographer — telescope viewing, a Milky Way group photo, plus parkas, chairs and hot chocolate (there's a 'star guarantee'). It's a memorable splurge, but a pricey, late (8:30pm+) outing ~1.5 hr away with temps near 40°F — best for the adults and older kids; a tough call with grandma and the 6-year-old.

reviews.quote: Absolutely breathtaking — an unforgettable experience. Our guide was incredibly knowledgeable and the views of the night sky were stunning.
It absolutely delivers — James, Shannon and Dylan went above and beyond. Just be ready to drive to different dark-sky spots in the dark.

events.name: Hawaiian Culture & Lei Making — Ohana Ranch (Airbnb)
events.drive: ~1 hr 40 min · south toward the volcano (Mountain View, Hilo side)
events.blurb: A culture-and-lei workshop at a family-run ranch in Mountain View: learn the meaning of lei, gather ti leaves and weave your own, and hear stories about the ʻāina. Honest heads-up — it's adults-only (18+), a working ranch still in early development (you may get muddy), and ~1.5 hr away on the east side, so it doesn't fit a near-home family day and likely isn't worth the drive for your group.

reviews.quote: Our guide was personable and knowledgeable and made the experience memorable. Highly recommend to those interested in the culture.
Family-run, good vibes — but probably half the time we weren't doing lei making, and the only material was ti leaves. Still developing as an attraction.

events.name: July 4th Fireworks Cruise — Body Glove (Kanoa II)
events.booking: ⚠ Appears SOLD OUT for Jul 4, 2026 · waitlist maybe (don't count on it)
events.blurb:
A festive on-the-water way to skip the land crowds for the July 4th fireworks — Body Glove's 65-ft double-decker catamaran, the Kanoa II, departs Kailua Pier and positions in the bay for an up-close view when the barge fireworks launch at 8pm. Includes a BBQ/American buffet, full open bar and live entertainment, with restrooms and shaded deck seating — comfortable for the whole multigen group.

⚠ Update: the July 4, 2026 cruise currently looks SOLD OUT. There may be a waitlist, but don't count on it (call 800-551-8911 to check).

reviews.quote: A festive, well-run evening — BBQ buffet, open bar and live music on a big stable catamaran, positioned for a great view when the fireworks go off over the bay.
The double-decker Kanoa II is roomy with restrooms and shaded seating — comfortable for families and grandparents.

events.name: July 4th Fireworks Super-Raft — Kona Explorer
events.ages: Better for adults & older kids (raft ride)
events.booking: ⚠ Appears SOLD OUT for Jul 4, 2026 · ~1.5 hrs
events.blurb:
A more intimate ~1.5-hr fireworks cruise on a 41-ft super-raft along the Kona coast, with a light dinner and drinks and an up-close view of the bay fireworks. Fun and personal, but it's a firmer raft ride with limited facilities — better for the adults and older kids than grandma and the 6-year-old.

⚠ Update: the July 4, 2026 cruise currently looks SOLD OUT.

reviews.quote: An intimate small-group raft cruise along the Kona coast with a light dinner and drinks, up close for the fireworks over Kailua Bay.
Fun and personal, but it's a firmer raft ride with limited facilities — better for adventurous guests than little kids or grandparents.

items.note: Land 12:33pm, stock up on the way south
Sunset & toes in the sand near home
Grandma's saimin on the way back
Casual local dinner in Kealakekua (open daily)
Honey tasting & open-hive demo — kids love it

items.note: Oceanfront table to watch the fireworks
Parade 6pm · fireworks 8pm — or a quieter vantage (tap for spots)
BOOKED — dry reef tour, check in at noon
Family brewpub — pizza & a kids' play space

days.note: Flight UA 1722 departs KOA 12:00pm — pack & head out early.

North beach then town for fireworks

items.note: Oceanfront table to watch the fireworks
Parade 6pm · fireworks 8pm — or a quieter vantage (tap for spots)

items.note: Comfort food & famous pies in Naʻalehu, on the drive back

days.note: Flight UA 1722 departs KOA 12:00pm — relaxed morning, pack & go.

items.note: Settle in, sunset swim near home
Oceanfront table to watch the fireworks

items.note: Comfort food & famous pies in Naʻalehu, on the drive back

itineraries.name: Volcano the 4th
itineraries.vibe: Volcano on July 4th · skip the fireworks

days.drive: Airport → lunch → Safeway → estate (~1 hr 15 min)

items.note: Land at Kona International, grab the rental car
Oceanfront lunch right after landing (~20 min from KOA)
Stock the house + grab an easy first-night dinner, then drive south
Kamakana playground — the wooden castle, for the kids
Fresh loaves — open till 2pm, goes fast
Grab poke bowls to eat back at the house
Back up to Captain Cook — tacos on the ocean-view patio
Original Hawaiian Chocolate tour — reserve ahead (Tue–Fri only)
Real-fruit shave ice at the ranch store — minutes from home

days.drive: South route — long day (~1 hr 30 min each way)
days.note: Skipping the Kailua fireworks for the volcano. The park is open 24/7 — you could catch the after-dark crater glow before the long drive home.

items.note: Comfort food & famous pies in Naʻalehu
Oceanfront brunch on Aliʻi Dr — minutes from the pier check-in
BOOKED — check in at noon, Kailua Pier · same spot as the luau
Stroll the Aliʻi Drive shops & galleries before the luau
